anchor:
Hey team! 🕵️‍♂️ Looking for a **Mid‑Level Frontend Engineer** (React, TypeScript, Tailwind, GraphQL) to hop on our remote squad (US wide, no time‑zone limits). 3‑5 yrs in building sleek, reusable UI components for a media SaaS. Must vibe with CI/CD, CSS‑in‑JS, and be a self‑starter handy with testing (Jest + React‑Testing‑Library). 💻💪

⚡️ **Salary**: $80K‑$95K, 100% full‑time, start ASAP. No security clearance needed. Drop portfolio/portfolio‑style repo, and your favorite component library prefs. Let’s lock something in!

positive.summary: Mid-Level Frontend Engineer with 4 years of experience building reusable UI components for media SaaS, proficient in React, TypeScript, Tailwind, and GraphQL.

work_experience.description: Led development of reusable component library for appointment scheduling platform using React, TypeScript, Tailwind, and GraphQL. Implemented unit and integration tests with Jest and React Testing Library; integrated CI/CD pipelines with GitHub Actions and Docker.

Built media SaaS dashboard for real-time ride data visualization using React, TypeScript, Tailwind, and GraphQL. Focused on component scalability and performance optimization. Maintained CI/CD pipeline with GitHub Actions and Docker.

projects.name: MediaKit
projects.description: Reusable component library for media SaaS built with React and TypeScript, styled with Tailwind and styled-components.

projects.name: RideDashboard
projects.description: Real-time dashboard for Swvl data, built with React, TypeScript, GraphQL, and Tailwind.

projects.name: GraphQL Playground
projects.description: Custom GraphQL client UI for internal use, using React and TypeScript.

negative.summary: Mid-level Site Reliability Engineer with 4.6 years of experience building and maintaining multi-cluster Kubernetes infrastructures using Terraform and Go.

work_experience.description: Implemented CI/CD pipelines with Terraform and Docker to streamline application deployments. Monitored services with Prometheus and Alertmanager, reducing incident response time by 35%. Contributed to a Go-based microservice that handled real-time transit routing.

Lead the automation of multi-cluster Kubernetes deployments across three regions using Terraform. Built and maintained gRPC-based service discovery in Go, achieving 99.9% uptime and reducing infra costs by 22% through efficient resource provisioning.

projects.name: ClusterCost
projects.description: Developed a cost-metric dashboard that aggregates Kubernetes cluster usage from AWS CloudWatch, providing actionable insights for cost optimization.

projects.name: GRPC-Auth
projects.description: Built a lightweight Go-based gRPC service for token-based authentication used across microservices in the care platform.

projects.name: Infra-Blueprint
projects.description: Designed reusable Terraform modules for deploying multi-region Kubernetes clusters, enabling rapid environment spin-up for QA and production.

certifications: Google Cloud Professional Cloud DevOps Engineer

anchor:
Hey everyone, I’m on the lookout for a **Senior Android Engineer** (5‑7 yrs of experience) who can help us revamp our core app stack.  
**Must‑have skill set**  
- **Kotlin 1.9** + **Jetpack Compose** (1.4+) for all UI work, with a strong grasp of **Material‑3** styling.  
- Architecture: Clean Architecture with **Dagger‑Hilt** for DI, **MVVM**, **Kotlin Coroutines**, **Flow**, and **State‑Flow** for reactive streams.  
- Persistence layer built on **Room** (1.4+), with **SQLite** migrations and type converters.  
- Networking via **Retrofit 2.9**, **OkHttp 4.11**, and **Moshi** for JSON, including a custom **Interceptor** for request signing.  
- **Gradle 8.1** with **Kotlin DSL** scripts, and **Android SDK 34** (API 34) targeting, with full compatibility to API 26+ via backward‑compatible libraries.  
- **Google Play Store v5** APIs: in‑app billing, subscription management, and the **Play Integrity API**.  
- **Firebase** integration (Cloud‑Messaging, Remote‑Config, Crashlytics) **plus** native push implementation.  
- Basic knowledge of **Kotlin Multiplatform Mobile** optional, but not required.

**Role focus**  
- Lead the implementation of a new modular architecture (feature modules).  
- Mentor a junior team of 2‑3 developers.  
- Conduct code reviews, set up CI/CD with **Google’s Cloud Build** + **Android Build Gradle** plugin.  
- Write unit tests (JUnit 5) + instrumentation tests (Espresso + UI‑Automator), integrating with **JUnit 5 + Robolectric**.  

**Constraints**  
- Full‑time, on‑site at our HQ (NYC/SE)?  – remote is *not* an option.  
- You must be available to start in the **second month of Q4 2026**.  
- Must hold a **Bachelor’s in Computer Science** (or equivalent) *or* a certified Android developer credential.  

Drop your contact or send me a link to your portfolio/resume if you match the criteria. Looking forward to building something great together!

positive.summary: Senior Android Engineer with 7 years of experience leading modular architecture and Jetpack Compose implementation for high‑traffic MENA tech apps. Proven track record in Clean Architecture, Dagger‑Hilt DI, MVVM, Kotlin Coroutines, and Play Store APIs. Willing to relocate to New York for an on‑site full‑time role.

work_experience.description: Led the revamp of Careem’s core ride‑hailing app by introducing a modular architecture with feature modules, Jetpack Compose UI aligned with Material‑3, and Clean Architecture. Designed and implemented reactive data flows using Kotlin Coroutines, Flow, and State‑Flow. Built robust persistence with Room and SQLite migrations, and integrated Retrofit, OkHttp, and Moshi with custom signing interceptors. Established CI/CD pipelines using Google Cloud Build and Gradle 8.1, and mentored a junior team of 3 developers.

Architect and lead the implementation of a feature‑module‑based e‑commerce app for Noon. Spearheaded the transition to Compose UI, ensuring backward compatibility with API 26+. Implemented in‑app billing, subscription management, and Play Integrity API for secure purchases. Developed end‑to‑end CI/CD using Google Cloud Build integrated with Android Build Gradle plugin. Conducted code reviews, provided mentorship to 2 junior engineers, and performed comprehensive unit, instrumentation, and Robolectric testing.

projects.name: SmartRide
projects.description: A modular ride‑hailing app built with Jetpack Compose, Clean Architecture, and Material‑3. Implemented advanced navigation, real‑time trip tracking, and secure payment flow with Play Store APIs.

projects.name: ShopEasy
projects.description: Feature‑module‑based e‑commerce Android app with deep linking, in‑app purchases, and dynamic theming. Integrated Google Cloud Build CI/CD and conducted extensive testing with JUnit 5 and Espresso.

negative.summary: ML Ops Engineer with 4+ years of experience building Docker-based deployment pipelines for PyTorch models on Kubeflow and GCP.

work_experience.description: Developed data pipelines and feature stores for recommendation models and built microservices for model inference.
Lead the design of automated deployment pipelines for PyTorch models using Docker containers, Kubeflow on GCP, and CI/CD workflows using GitLab CI.

projects.name: PyTorch Model Deployment Pipeline
projects.description: Automated pipeline that builds Docker images, sculpts Kubeflow models, and deploys them to GCP Kubernetes clusters with zero-downtime updates.

projects.name: Real-time Inference Microservice
projects.description: A lightweight microservice written in Python that serves PyTorch models via gRPC, containerized with Docker and orchestrated on Kubernetes.

projects.name: ML Ops Toolkit
projects.description: Collection of reusable CI/CD templates and Helm charts for deploying ML models with monitoring, using Kubeflow and Prometheus.

certifications: Google Cloud Professional Machine Learning Engineer

anchor:
We’re on a growth sprint and need a **Senior Frontend Lead** to own our UI architecture, mentor the squad, and ship feature‑complete pages that run land‑fast on every device.

- **Role & seniority**: Lead Frontend Engineer / Architect – 5+ years in production, proven in scaling an app from prototype to 500k monthly active users.  
- **Tech stack**: React 18+, Next.js 13+ (SSG, ISR, Middleware), Tailwind CSS v3, CSS‑in‑JS (styled‑components or emotion – pick the one you love), TypeScript, webpack 5, and Vercel for deployments.  
- **Core responsibilities**:  
  - Design and evolve a reusable, component‑driven design system that spans web and mobile web.  
  - Define and enforce code‑quality gates (ESLint/Prettier, commit hooks), unit+integration tests (Jest, React‑Testing‑Library) and CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, Docker).  
  - Champion performance: page‑speed budgets, code‑splitting, bundle analysis, lighthouse reviews, and image optimization.  
  - Lead accessibility (WCAG 2.1) audits, ensuring WC and inclusive design is built “in‑band.”  
  - Guide the team through technical debt reduction and refactoring micro‑frontend boundaries.  
  - Mentor 3–5 mid‑level developers, conduct code reviews, pair‑program, and run regular knowledge‑sharing sessions.  
- **Desired experience**:  
  - Proven track record of shipping Next.js applications at scale (SSR, CSR, ISR).  
  - Strong grasp of serverless or edge functions (Vercel, Netlify, Cloudflare Workers).  
  - Experience with state‑management patterns (Context API, Redux Toolkit, Zustand) and data‑fetching libraries (React‑Query, SWR).  
  - Comfortable with cloud services – AWS (Amplify, S3, CloudFront) or GCP; serverless backends, CI/CD monitoring, and basic DevOps.  
  - Open to contributing to API‑first initiatives, publishing reusable NPM packages, or maintaining performance dashboards (New Relic, LogRocket).  
- **Qualifications**: Bachelor’s + 5‑year professional record (or equivalent). Excellent written & spoken English; fluent in English‑first environments.  
- **Location**: San Francisco Bay Area – full‑time hybrid (2 days onsite per week). Remote work allowed during onboarding.  
- **Contract**: Permanent, not C2C; start within 3‑4 weeks of interview.  
- **Bonus**: Prior startup or scale‑up experience, leadership of cross‑functional squads, certifications (AWS Certified Developer or similar).  

If you’re a senior frontend expert who can weave architecture, performance, and mentorship into the same sprint, let’s talk about how you can help us ship the best web experience yet.

positive.summary: Lead Frontend Engineer with 5.5 years of experience driving UI architecture, performance, and mentorship for high‑traffic web applications.

work_experience.description: Built and maintained key commerce pages using React and Next.js, implemented server‑less edge functions on Vercel, and integrated state‑management with Redux Toolkit and React‑Query. Contributed to performance budgets and accessibility audits.

Owned the UI architecture for a Next.js 13 web platform that grew from prototype to 500k+ MAU. Designed a reusable component library with Tailwind and styled‑components, enforced code‑quality gates, led a squad of 5 mid‑level engineers, and championed performance and accessibility across web and mobile web.

projects.name: NextStatePortal
projects.description: High‑traffic portal built with Next.js 13, ISR, and serverless middleware on Vercel. Implemented advanced data fetching with React‑Query and state management via Redux Toolkit, achieving 0.85s average LCP.

Enforced runtime policies with OPA, performed security scanning with Snyk, and automated compliance checks to meet industry standards.

anchor: We’re scaling fast and need a *mid‑level Machine Learning Engineer* who can ship models to production at scale. Think TensorFlow + BERT + Apache Spark, not PyTorch. Look for someone with 2–4 years of experience in building data pipelines, training and tuning deep‑learning models, and deploying them on Kubernetes or similar container platforms. They should be comfortable writing clean Python code, querying data with SQL or Spark‑SQL, and monitoring model performance with Prometheus or Grafana. Speaking fluent English and French is a plus. We’re open to remote work or offices in the EU and US, full‑time with a competitive salary and equity, and a 3‑month start date. No security clearance required at this stage.

Lead deployment of recommendation models on Kubernetes, monitored model drift with Prometheus and Grafana, and optimized inference latency.

anchor:
Looking for an **Senior RTOS Kernel Engineer** (C/C++) – 8‑12 years of embedded experience, specialist in **FreeRTOS 10.x** and **Zephyr 5.0**, with deep knowledge of **ARM Cortex‑M** (M4/M7) interrupt handling, tick‑less scheduling, and low‑power mode optimization. Must have demonstrated ability to develop and refine kernel APIs, portables, and device drivers for real‑time multicore systems.  

Required toolchain: **IAR Embedded Workbench 9.51** (or Keil MDK‑ARM NuCLEO‑F4), **OpenOCD 0.11.0** for JTAG debugging, and **Segger Embedded Studio 2023.1** for code analysis.  

Project scope includes:  
- Porting and patching a custom microkernel to support a new ARM‑Cortex‑M7 DSP core.  
- Implementing advanced power‑management features (dynamic voltage scaling) and profiling with **Percepio µTimeTrace**.  
- Integrating the kernel with a proprietary real‑time IPC mechanism and developing a full demo firmware for an automotive safety‑critical module.  

Location: **Denver, CO** (on‑site), full‑time, permanent. Competitive salary (USD $120‑$150 k) plus benefits. Immediate start preferred.Please contact with updated résumé and portfolio of kernel contributions.

positive.summary: Senior RTOS Kernel Engineer with 10 years of embedded experience, specialist in FreeRTOS 10.x and Zephyr 5.0, deep expertise in ARM Cortex‑M4/M7 interrupt handling, tick‑less scheduling, and low‑power optimization.

work_experience.description: Developed lightweight RTOS modules for mobile bug reporting, optimized interrupt handling and low-power modes on ARM Cortex-M4 cores using IAR Embedded Workbench 9.51, and facilitated debugging with OpenOCD.

Led porting of FreeRTOS 10.x and Zephyr 5.0 to custom ARM Cortex-M7 DSP cores, implemented tick‑less scheduling, dynamic voltage scaling, and low‑power optimization for automotive safety‑critical modules.

Architected and integrated a custom microkernel with a proprietary IPC mechanism, delivered full demo firmware for automotive safety‑critical module, and maintained kernel APIs, portables, and device drivers for real‑time multicore systems.

anchor:
We are expanding our cyber resilience portfolio and seeking an **Enterprise Cybersecurity Architect – Zero Trust & Cloud Security**. The ideal candidate will have **8‑12 years** of progressive experience designing and implementing end‑to‑end Zero‑Trust frameworks across multi‑cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GCP), advanced threat protection (XDR, EDR), and secure DevSecOps pipelines. Proficiency in **Okta Identity Cloud, Azure AD Conditional Access, Google Workspace Security Center**, and **Next‑Gen Firewalls (Palo Alto Networks, Fortinet)** is essential.  

Candidates must hold **Certified Information Systems Security Professional (CISSP)** or **ISO 27001 Lead Implementation** credentials, with a track record of delivering security architecture for regulated industries (PCI‑DSS, HIPAA, GDPR). Strong expertise in **Risk Assessment (CRAMM, FAIR), Governance, Risk & Compliance (GRC)**, and a proven ability to translate business objectives into robust technical controls will be highly valued.  

We offer a **senior leadership role** with a focus on shaping our security strategy, mentoring a multi‑disciplinary team, and delivering measurable risk reduction for high‑profile clients. Positions are available in our **New York City** headquarters or as a **remote** engagement for qualified candidates. The engagement can be an immediate full‑time placement or a 6‑12 month contract with C2C at a competitive rate. If you thrive on architecting resilient, future‑proof defenses and can drive cross‑functional innovation at scale, we invite you to apply.

positive.summary: Enterprise Cybersecurity Architect with 12 years experience in Zero‑Trust and multi‑cloud security for regulated industries.

work_experience.description: Developed security controls for GDPR compliance, performed risk assessments using CRAMM and FAIR, and supported Zero‑Trust initiatives.
Architected PCI‑DSS compliant Zero‑Trust framework across multi‑cloud environments, integrated XDR/EDR solutions, and established secure DevSecOps pipelines.
Designed and implemented end‑to‑end Zero‑Trust architecture across AWS, Azure, and GCP for a HIPAA‑compliant healthcare platform, leading a cross‑functional team of 12 security engineers.

projects.description: Automated tool for Zero‑Trust policy distribution across AWS, Azure, and GCP environments.

projects.description: Built a CI/CD pipeline integrating security scans, SAST, DAST, and container hardening for multi‑cloud services.

certifications: Palo Alto Networks Certified Network Security Engineer (PCNSE)

Deployed with Docker, GitHub Actions CI/CD, and Vercel preview URLs for each PR.

Led frontend team for subscription service platform, implementing Next.js 13 App Router, Edge Middleware on Node 18, ISR for large product catalogs, and micro-frontend architecture via Module Federation. Automated UI tests with Vitest, @testing-library/react, and Playwright, achieved Lighthouse 90+ score enforcement, and integrated Segment and Snowplow analytics. Designed Figma-to-code automation for Chakra UI and Tailwind components, maintaining WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility.

Developed and optimized e-commerce front for subscription-based model using Next.js 13 App Router and React 18, incorporating Tailwind CSS 3.3 and Chakra UI 5.5. Implemented performance tuning with webpack 5, dynamic imports, and strategic cache-throttle patterns with SWR. Built CI/CD pipeline with GitHub Actions, Dockerized staging environment, and deployed to Vercel with custom domain and SSL. Conducted automated UI testing with Vitest and Playwright, ensuring 95% code coverage. Maintained accessibility compliance (WCAG 2.1 AA).

anchor:
**Mid‑Level QA Automation Engineer – Playwright/Python Focus (Python 3.11, Playwright 1.35)**  

- **Experience & Expertise**  
  - 3–5 years of QA automation in a SaaS or micro‑services environment.  
  - Deep knowledge of **Playwright** (latest LTS, 1.35.x) for browser automation, with experience building reusable page object models.  
  - Proficiency in **Python 3.11** and **pytest** (with pytest‑bdd or Allure reporting).  
  - Comfortable writing and maintaining API integration tests against **GraphQL** endpoints and RESTful services (Postman/Newman, Insomnia, or pure Python `requests`).  
  - Familiar with **Post‑greSQL** and **MongoDB** for test data provisioning and validation.

- **Tools & Technologies**  
  - CI/CD: **GitHub Actions** (Python actions, Docker layers), with optional Jenkins fallback.  
  - Containerization: **Docker** for test harnesses and data services; **K3s** or kind for quick cluster setup.  
  - Version control: **Git** (GitHub, GitLab, Azure DevOps).  
  - Test management: **TestRail 2024** (or alternative).  
  - Defect tracking: **Jira 2024** (with custom fields for BDD scenarios).  
  - Performance testing: **Locust** 2.x for simulated load on APIs.  
  - Static analysis & coverage: **SonarQube 10**; **coverage.py** plugged into GitHub Actions to enforce 90 % module coverage.  

- **Key Responsibilities**  
  - Design, implement, and maintain **end‑to‑end** Playwright test suites covering UI + GraphQL flows.  
  - Build and version a **Playwright test data factory** that can spin up tenant data using Docker Compose and a fixture-driven approach.  
  - Integrate tests into the **GitHub Actions** pipeline, generating comprehensive reports uploaded to Azure DevOps Analytics.  
  - Collaborate with backend devs to specify **contract‑based tests** for GraphQL mutations and queries.  
  - Participate in **Agile ceremonies** (sprint planning, retrospectives) and write test acceptance criteria.  
  - Mentor junior test engineers on Playwright best practices and Python code quality.  

- **Qualifications**  
  - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or related field.  
  - ISTQB Foundation or Advanced certification preferred.  
  - Strong debugging, problem‑solving, and analytical skills; comfortable using DevTools, Wireshark, and browser console diagnostics.  
  - Excellent communication skills and the ability to explain complex test logic to non‑technical stakeholders.  
  - Experience with **cloud infrastructure** (AWS EKS or Azure AKS) is a plus.  

- **Logistics**  
  - Remote‑first, with occasional travel (up to 10 %) to offices in the **US East Coast**.  
  - Start date: Immediate (full‑time, C2C not permitted).  

- **Security & Compliance**  
  - Knowledge of **SOC 2 Type II** and **ISO 27001** testing requirements is a bonus.  
  - Ability to maintain **R‑AM, N‑AP, or U‑VPN** access for internal tools; higher clearance (S3, IAM) acceptable for compliance projects.

positive.summary: QA Automation Engineer with 5 years experience building end‑to‑end Playwright tests for SaaS and micro‑services applications.

work_experience.description: Implemented manual and automated regression tests for the Careem rider app, focusing on API reliability and UI consistency.
Built end‑to‑end Playwright test suites (v1.35) covering UI flows and GraphQL endpoints, integrated tests into GitHub Actions CI pipeline, and mentored junior testers.
Designed reusable page object models for Swvl’s web platform, created a Playwright test data factory using Docker Compose, and automated contract‑based GraphQL tests with pytest fixtures.

projects.name: Swvl Playwright Suite
projects.description: End‑to‑end browser automation suite covering UI, GraphQL mutations, and REST APIs for the Swvl web platform.

projects.description: Docker‑based test data factory that spins up tenant data in PostgreSQL and MongoDB for isolated test runs.

projects.name: GraphQL Contract Tests
projects.description: Automated contract tests using pytest‑bdd to validate GraphQL query and mutation contracts against Vezeeta backend.

projects.name: Locust Load Test for API
projects.description: Simulated load tests on Vezeeta’s GraphQL endpoints to evaluate performance under high concurrency.

work_experience.description: Architected and delivered enterprise‑grade smart contract suites for Layer‑2 ecosystems, led a team of 8 engineers, implemented cross‑chain bridge protocols, and established a rigorous security review pipeline.

Leading a cross‑functional team to deliver multi‑asset cross‑chain bridge solutions across Polygon, Arbitrum, and zk‑Rollups, integrating automated verification with MythX, Hardhat, and Foundry, and driving code‑review standards.

anchor:
**Junior Embedded Firmware Engineer – ARM Cortex‑M4/Cortex‑M7, FreeRTOS 10.4.x – 100 % Remote (Occasional On‑Site in Boise, ID)**  

We are looking for an entry‑level embedded firmware engineer with a passion for low‑power, high‑reliability microcontroller development. The role focuses on the STM32H7/H5 Ultra‑High‑Performance Cortex‑M7/M4 families and requires proficiency in the free‑RTOS ecosystem, modern IDE tooling, and foundational safety best‑practices.  

**Key Technical Requirements**  
- **Programming:** C “plain” with extensive use of CMSIS‑V5 and the STM32Cube HAL libraries; no C++ required.  
- **RTOS:** FreeRTOS v10.4.x (kernel, kernel extensions, kernel debug libraries). Must be able to configure task priorities, stack usage, and tickless idle.  
- **Toolchain:**  
  - ARM Cortex‑M *standard* toolchain: arm-none-eabi-gcc 9.3.1, with Makefiles or CMake‑based build systems.  
  - Integrated development: STM32CubeIDE 1.5.0 or later (Eclipse‑based), with STM32CubeMX 4 or later for peripheral configuration and code generation.  
  - Debugger: Segger J‑Link V6.0+ (or equivalent SWD debugger), STM32CubeProgrammer for flashing.  
  - Version control & CI: Git (GitHub), GitLab CI, or Azure DevOps Repos with a pipeline that builds, runs static analysis (e.g., cppcheck 2.6), and flashes a test board.  
- **Hardware:**  
  - STM32H743/VL/ZIx (Cortex‑M7) or STM32H750 (dual‑core Cortex‑M4/M7) evaluation boards.  
  - Integrated DC‑DC converters and power monitoring – we expect a basic understanding of current‑sense ADCs and fault‑detect routines.  
  - Peripheral familiarity: UART, SPI, I²C, CAN, USB‑FS/HS, SDIO, Timers, DMA, and the core power‑management peripherals.  
- **Embedded Protocols & Security:**  
  - Basic knowledge of the mbedTLS 2.28 library for Bluetooth‑LE Secure Connections, Wi‑Fi TLS‑v1.3, and DTLS.  
  - Familiarity with the ARM TrustZone for Cortex‑M7 (optional, but a plus).  
- **Safety & Standards:**  
  - Understanding of ISO 26262 fundamentals (functional safety concept, safety lifecycle) is beneficial.  
  - Knowledge of MISRA‑C:2012 guidelines, at least in the “Severity A” and “Severity B” categories.  
- **Soft Skills:**  
  - Demonstrated ability to write self‑documenting code and unit‑tests (Unity test framework).  
  - Strong problem‑solving, debugging using GDB, and evidence of a collaborative mindset (code reviews, version control etiquette).

**Pipeline & Deliverables**  
1. **Initial screen:** Pull requests mergeable under the “ci‑ready” label.  
2. **Sprint demo:** Weekly “kernel‑level demo” via video call, showcasing a custom peripheral driver with activity‑queue handling under FreeRTOS.  
3. **Optional assessment:** Write a 3‑task scheduler (one for UART reception, one for heart‑beat timers, one for DMA‑co‑ordinated flash writes) in an 8‑tier stack, ensuring < 64 bytes stack usage per task.  

**Compensation & Schedule**  
- **Rate:** $28–$32 / hr (remote).  
- **Hours:** 20 hrs / wk (split across EEZ of Boise).  
- **Travel:** < 5 % of time for on‑site sprint reviews and team huddles.  

**How to Apply**  
Send a résumé with a concise “cvs” overview section, posting a sample of a small HW‑driver module (.c/.h) or a GitHub repo with a minimal FreeRTOS build. Applications should be submitted by **17 Nov 2026**.

positive.summary: Entry-level embedded firmware engineer with 1.3 years of experience in STM32 development, FreeRTOS 10.4.x, and low-power design on STM32H7/H5 platforms.

work_experience.description: Implemented current‑sense ADC driver for DC‑DC converter monitoring on STM32H750 dual‑core board. Leveraged HAL ADC with DMA, interrupt routines, and FreeRTOS queue integration. Applied MISRA‑C:2012 guidelines, designed Unity unit tests, and used Segger J‑Link for debugging. Maintained GitHub repository with GitLab CI for static analysis and automated flashing using STM32CubeProgrammer.

Developed low‑power UART and DMA drivers for the order collection device, utilizing STM32Cube HAL, CMSIS, FreeRTOS 10.4.x, and ensuring <64‑byte stack per task. Configured STM32H743 board with STM32CubeMX, implemented tickless idle and power‑management features, validated code quality with cppcheck and Unity unit tests, and managed continuous integration via GitLab CI. Debugged with Segger J-Link and flashed with STM32CubeProgrammer.

anchor:
**Junior Embedded Firmware Engineer – ARM Cortex‑M4/Cortex‑M7, FreeRTOS 10.4.x – 100 % Remote (Occasional On‑Site in Boise, ID)**  

We are looking for an entry‑level embedded firmware engineer with a passion for low‑power, high‑reliability microcontroller development. The role focuses on the STM32H7/H5 Ultra‑High‑Performance Cortex‑M7/M4 families and requires proficiency in the free‑RTOS ecosystem, modern IDE tooling, and foundational safety best‑practices.  

**Key Technical Requirements**  
- **Programming:** C “plain” with extensive use of CMSIS‑V5 and the STM32Cube HAL libraries; no C++ required.  
- **RTOS:** FreeRTOS v10.4.x (kernel, kernel extensions, kernel debug libraries). Must be able to configure task priorities, stack usage, and tickless idle.  
- **Toolchain:**  
  - ARM Cortex‑M *standard* toolchain: arm-none-eabi-gcc 9.3.1, with Makefiles or CMake‑based build systems.  
  - Integrated development: STM32CubeIDE 1.5.0 or later (Eclipse‑based), with STM32CubeMX 4 or later for peripheral configuration and code generation.  
  - Debugger: Segger J‑Link V6.0+ (or equivalent SWD debugger), STM32CubeProgrammer for flashing.  
  - Version control & CI: Git (GitHub), GitLab CI, or Azure DevOps Repos with a pipeline that builds, runs static analysis (e.g., cppcheck 2.6), and flashes a test board.  
- **Hardware:**  
  - STM32H743/VL/ZIx (Cortex‑M7) or STM32H750 (dual‑core Cortex‑M4/M7) evaluation boards.  
  - Integrated DC‑DC converters and power monitoring – we expect a basic understanding of current‑sense ADCs and fault‑detect routines.  
  - Peripheral familiarity: UART, SPI, I²C, CAN, USB‑FS/HS, SDIO, Timers, DMA, and the core power‑management peripherals.  
- **Embedded Protocols & Security:**  
  - Basic knowledge of the mbedTLS 2.28 library for Bluetooth‑LE Secure Connections, Wi‑Fi TLS‑v1.3, and DTLS.  
  - Familiarity with the ARM TrustZone for Cortex‑M7 (optional, but a plus).  
- **Safety & Standards:**  
  - Understanding of ISO 26262 fundamentals (functional safety concept, safety lifecycle) is beneficial.  
  - Knowledge of MISRA‑C:2012 guidelines, at least in the “Severity A” and “Severity B” categories.  
- **Soft Skills:**  
  - Demonstrated ability to write self‑documenting code and unit‑tests (Unity test framework).  
  - Strong problem‑solving, debugging using GDB, and evidence of a collaborative mindset (code reviews, version control etiquette).

**Pipeline & Deliverables**  
1. **Initial screen:** Pull requests mergeable under the “ci‑ready” label.  
2. **Sprint demo:** Weekly “kernel‑level demo” via video call, showcasing a custom peripheral driver with activity‑queue handling under FreeRTOS.  
3. **Optional assessment:** Write a 3‑task scheduler (one for UART reception, one for heart‑beat timers, one for DMA‑co‑ordinated flash writes) in an 8‑tier stack, ensuring < 64 bytes stack usage per task.  

**Compensation & Schedule**  
- **Rate:** $28–$32 / hr (remote).  
- **Hours:** 20 hrs / wk (split across EEZ of Boise).  
- **Travel:** < 5 % of time for on‑site sprint reviews and team huddles.  

**How to Apply**  
Send a résumé with a concise “cvs” overview section, posting a sample of a small HW‑driver module (.c/.h) or a GitHub repo with a minimal FreeRTOS build. Applications should be submitted by **17 Nov 2026**.
